source: A Comprehensive Survey on Graph Neural Networks

concepts.finding: Comprehensive survey covering GNN architectures: GCN, GAT, GraphSAGE, GIN, and variants. Identifies key design dimensions: aggregator type (mean, max, attention), depth (oversmoothing beyond 2-3 layers), and training approaches (supervised, unsupervised, self-supervised). Recurrent vs. convolutional vs. attention-based GNNs compared.
concepts.implication: GNN design space provides multiple approaches for embedding keyword co-occurrence graphs. Key decisions: neighbor aggregation method (attention-based may capture intent relevance), number of layers (2-3 sufficient for keyword neighborhoods), and whether to use inductive (GraphSAGE) or transductive (GCN) approaches.
concepts.open_question: Which GNN architecture is most appropriate for keyword-intent graphs with millions of nodes? Can attention-based aggregation identify intent-relevant neighbors?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A 2-layer Graph Attention Network on keyword co-occurrence graphs produces better intent clusters than GCN or GraphSAGE due to learned neighbor importance weighting.
